summary: Well-structured test suite with strong core coverage of main functions (version parsing, bump type detection, version stepping, changelog generation, file updates). Good use of fixtures and integration tests. Pester idioms used correctly with proper cleanup and independence. Weaknesses: missing direct unit test for Read-CommitsFile, limited edge case coverage (e.g., prerelease versions, extreme numbers, empty commit messages), and lightweight validation of changelog structure. Tests validate against realistic fixture data and would catch most regressions in primary code paths, but some corner cases could slip through.
